summary: Crystal‑clear Betty Board capture of a legendary ’77 show with a smoking Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence and tight playing from the whole band.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard (Betty Board) source – crisp, well‑balanced and free of noticeable flaws. Musically the concert shines: the second set is described as “epic,” highlighted by a blistering Help > Slip > Franklin’s Tower, tight Terrapin Station, a standout Eyes of the World bass solo and one of the best Playin’ in the Band jams of ’77.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen detailed, mostly five‑star reviews praise both the audio fidelity and the performance; only a single outlier gives a low star rating without criticism of the music.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Board (BBB) mix praised as crisp, great balance and “great mix”; occasional listener confusion about matrix but no audible issues. No hiss, drop‑outs or balance problems reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers rave about a seamless, high‑energy second set that flows from Blues of Allah into Terrapin Station and then launches into the legendary Help>Slip>Franklin's sequence; overall pacing is described as “epic” and “tight throughout.”

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone and solos repeatedly called “well‑formed,” “blazing,” and “from his heart”; noted for plugging into a Moog synthesizer early in the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass praised for its flat‑picked Alembic punch; distinct solo on Eyes of the World highlighted as a rare moment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals and rhythm chime noted, especially on Sugaree where his vocal hits are singled out.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano described as “slamming,” “beautifully constructed phrasing,” and a key driver of the show’s energy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann described as tight, crisp, and driving the groove throughout.
